sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 1.1g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
sections.subtitleText: 1.1g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 60.1g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 60.1g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 25.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 25.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet.
sections.subtitleText: outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 37% daily value, magnesium at 30% daily value, zinc at 23% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 37% daily value, magnesium at 30% daily value, zinc at 23% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: one of the best budget staple foods. it is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples. very filling for the calories. the fibre and protein combo gives it strong practical fullness. usually affordable and practical. makes it easy to use as a real staple rather than a niche health food.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: contains some anti-nutrients. phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can be rough on some people digestively. not everyone handles legumes equally well. needs some cooking effort. a small practical friction versus more convenient foods.

closing.summary: Lentils has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
